title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Constable Trotter enters the study at Monkswell Manor to confront guests Christopher Lawn, Mrs. Boyle, Mr. Paravicini, and Colonel Metcalf regarding their potential connection to the Longridge Farm case. He warns them that one of them is in life-threatening danger. The guests deny any involvement; Mr. Paravicini claims ignorance of local history, Mrs. Boyle questions why she is being implicated in a murder, Christopher Lawn claims he was only a child at the time, and Colonel Metcalf states he only read about the incident in the newspaper while stationed in Edinburgh. Frustrated by their denials, Trotter leaves, warning that any subsequent deaths will be their own fault.

Following Trotter's departure, Christopher Lawn mocks the tension by whistling "Three Blind Mice" and making a joke about strangling Mrs. Boyle, which terrifies her. Colonel Metcalf then reveals to the others that Mrs. Boyle was the official responsible for arranging accommodations in 1940 and was the person who sent three children to Longridge Farm. Mrs. Boyle denies responsibility for the subsequent events.

Trotter returns to clarify that he is a constable rather than an inspector and intends to call Inspector Hogben. However, Molly Davis informs him that the telephone is broken. Suspecting the line has been cut, Trotter goes outside to investigate, followed by Jules. While Jules checks the upstairs switchboard, Mr. Paravicini plays "Three Blind Mice" on the piano. In a bedroom, Christopher Lawn transitions from whistling to sobbing before resolving to "finish it." Meanwhile, upstairs, Jules discovers a pink bus ticket that falls from Molly’s glove, leaving him in a state of disbelief.
